entry.title: F4 help for Application Job in Public Cloud (2502 release)
entry.content:
A much-awaited feature of public cloud is now available with the 2502 release, with this feature we can attach a custom F4 value help to application job parameters, a basic yet essential feature.

Technical Details

Below are the steps on how we can attach F4 value help to application job parameters.

1) Create a class where all the business logic will be executed, this class will also contain all the job parameters.

To schedule an application job, you require an application job catalog entry that contains the name of the class (Class with execute method) which contains the interface IF_APJ_RT_RUN. You can create and edit application job catalog entries in the editor for application job catalog entries.

 

2) Creation of Application Job Catalog Entries

You can create an application job catalog entry in the ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) using the creation wizard.

How to provide F4 to a parameter in application job

  • Create the above catalog entry for the field.
  • Select CDS view as value help type and provide the CDS view in the value help section.

Creation of Value help CDS

Create CDS view with below annotations and assign this view to a parameter of the corresponding job catalog. The application job framework uses these annotations to render the value help dialog and the CDS view to search, filter, and display the list of possible values.

  • @ObjectModel.dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP. This is a mandatory annotation for defining a CDS value help. Only CDS views with this annotation can be used in the application job framework.

Along with F4 help, now we can group the job parameters fields in different sections by creating different sections and groups.

Add Section and Group in catalog-->

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Add the group to each of the parameter 

Creation of Application Job Template

Create application job template and provide the catalog entry that was created in above steps

Application Job Fiori App to execute the job

User can select any value from the F4 that was attached in Job catalog to these fields.

Pretty easy right? Give it a try and let me know in the comments section whether this post was helpful. I will be creating more blogs on the new feature of public cloud that were introduced in the 2502 release.

Thanks

Abhi

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA for Poultry operations covering Breeder, Hatchery, Broiler and Feed Management
entry.content:
Implementing S/4 HANA in Poultry Industry

  1. Grand Parent Management
  2. Breeder Management
  3. Hatchery Management
  4. Broiler Management - Own and Contract Farming
  5. Chicken Processing
  6. Feed Mill Management
  7. Layer Management

Module features are as below along with SAP standards used

Breeder Management

Complete flock calendar generation for vertically linked management of operations from
Breeder, Hatchery and Broiler. Breeder Management covers entire life cycle from shed
placement till liquidation of the batch

Following matrix will detail out standard and customization involved

NoFeatureCustomizationS/4 HANA Transactions
1Flock CalendarFlock Calendar works as MRP for poultry operations across timeline - Screen was customizedComplete Customization
2Batch Schedule and PlacementCustomization for Shed ready activities including mobile AppStandard Procurement and material movement used with hierarchy as Plant and Bin for Batches placed in multiple sheds.
3Flock merging, Shed to shed transferNoStandard Material movement codes used
4Feed, Medicine, IndentValidations for demand addedStandard Inventory request and Material transfer codes used
5Daily TransactionCustomized Screen with mobile AppAccount posting for bird cost
Inventory movements with standard codes including material issue
6Egg CollectionCustomized Screen with mobile AppEgg cost calculation with account posting
Depreciation (Amortization) entries, Material receipt and Material issue for scrap for broken eggs
7Overhead expense Budget and loadingBudget was calculated for per day per bird and per day per egg basis based on flock calendar planningStandard budget and forecast screens were utilized for data inputs for budgets across indirect expense ledgers
8Flock Performance reportsCustomized for poultry requirements with breed standard charts as benchmarkStandard material movement tables and costing tables from CO used for cost

Hatchery Management

Manages 21 days life cycle of hatchery operations from setting to hatching. No calculations and calendars are required for your hatchery manager. Integration with Hatcher and Setter through data acquisition systems for seamless monitoring and alerts.

Hatchery

NoFeatureCustomizationS/4 HANA Transactions
1Setter and Hatcher planningThrough flock calendar linkNone
2Candling TestCustom screen for data entryEWM setup with inventory movements
3Breakout analysisCustom screen for data entryNone
4Hatchery integrationsConfigurationsSCWM/SENSOR (Sensor Data Integration)
5Hatchery performance reportsCustomized reports as per Industry specificsInventory MIS

Broiler Management

Track own farming and contract farming cycle in single solution. Manages multiple locations and farms with roles like lifting supervisor, line supervisor, manager etc. real time bird cost and analytics through reports.

Broiler features

NoFeaturesCustomizationS/4 HANA Transaction
1Shed readyFiori Mobile App for farmNA
2Placement scheduleDOC reservation with hatcheryWM and IM modules transactions
3Batch placementBatch placement screen with mortality and shed selectionIM, MM and WM module transactions
4Daily transactionFeed, Medicine, Vaccine consumption, along with mortality detail. Fiori Mobile App for farmMaterial issue, Material request transactions
5Brid sales and liftingCustomized screen for lifting schedule and weigh scale integrationSales order with catch weight management (CWM) for multiple UoM orders
6Growing charge calculationA/P invoice posting as per GC scheme calculations with incentives and deductions and MSP baseFI transactions with TDS impact
7Chicken weighmentIntegration with bluetooth scales for poultryDelivery / Dispatch transactions

Processing Plant Management

We have utilized standard SAP PP module as base to configure chicken processing operations.

Processing Plant Management helps production managers in planning, measuring and executing the production with least wastage, minimum cost and achieve high utilization of resources for better yield.

NoFeature
1Production forecast based on sales forecast
2Configure multiple BOM with multi level harvesting
4Identify shrinkage at each level of goods movement at multiple level
5Integration with devices for hanging weight to identify exact inputs
6Assign and configure wastage, weight ratio and cost ratios in
7Enable Quality and cost control at each step of process for better quality and quantity of yield.

Feed Mill Management

Feed Mill is and integral part of poultry farming, almost 70% expense of bird cost is driven by feed cost. PoultryOS enables integration with flocks to generate feed production planning with parameter based quality control and approval based material movement.

NoFeature
1Multiple formulas with semi finished and finished good as material for feed production
2Costing for overhead like labor, electricity and material handling
3Resource utilization and capacity utilization planning, track die utilization, machine utilization on daily and production order basis.
4Quality control management with test templates for each item from material inward till finished good consumption
5Production planning with sales and captive consumption forecast
6Production and costing analysis and MIS for tracking and control

With this solution we were able to show critical improvements in business operations and performance and utilization of SAP peaked. With Fiori Apps and BTP integration adoption of solution till last stakeholder like farm line and lifting supervisor was ensured.

Power of SAP put to use by industry experts for poultry not only highlights SAP's strength as a comprehensive operations framework of organizations but also shows its flexibility to adopt.

Sagar Godbole

Team LogicalDNA

SAP S/4HANASAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Streamlining direct material sourcing from Design to Contract- Secure collaboration
entry.content:
Engineering changes can be a profit killer. Delays can devastate time to market. The answer? Strategic partnerships with direct material suppliers who are engaged early in product design. Collaboration prevents rework, accelerates time to market and safeguards your bottom line. Without it, companies risk escalating costs, lost revenue and damaged reputations.

Manufacturers need a robust and collaborative direct sourcing platform to fully realize these benefits. It must enable intuitive, context-driven collaboration between internal teams (R&D, project management, manufacturing, quality) and supplier’s stakeholders, with ironclad security for sensitive business data.

SAP Ariba Direct Material Sourcing delivers this critical capability, streamlining sourcing from design to contract. The SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release enhances this further with native integration into SAP Integrated Product Development, enabling secure collaboration on engineering drawings and specifications—ultimately driving higher profit margins

Here is how the new capability works:

  1. Create secure internal collaboration by inviting cross functional experts to finalise the designs and specifications which needs to be shared with suppliers who gets invited into the sourcing process

 

 

2. Standard workflow which orchestrates finalisation of the drawings and specifications in the collaboration and notifies sourcing manager via status update to publish the sourcing project to invited suppliers

 

 

 

 

3. Invited suppliers can securely access the shared drawings and specifications via SAP Supplier quotation management (Supplier application) to fully understand the design requirements of the requested direct materials

 

4. Supplier’s can use markup for any clarifications which they need from buyer’s organisation

 

 

5. Right stakeholder from buyer’s organization provides clarifications intime to supplier and system takes care of keeping all the discussion intact

 

 

 

6. Supplier creates and submit the best quotation with the complete understanding of the requirements associated with every material quoted in the quotation

7. Sourcing manager along with other expert stakeholders reviews the supplier quotation to finalize the award decision. Buying organization stakeholders can use markups to clarify supplier response.

This new capability delivers significant benefits:

  • Faster Time-to-Market: Streamlined collaboration and automated workflows accelerate design finalization and sourcing processes.
  • Reduced Costs: Early collaboration prevents costly engineering changes and rework.
  • Improved Supplier Engagement: Secure access and clear communication enable suppliers to submit accurate and competitive bids.
  • Enhanced Quality: Thorough design reviews and clear specifications ensure high-quality products.
  • Increased Collaboration Efficiency: Secure platform fosters seamless collaboration between internal teams and suppliers.

entry.title: #11 - Understand the Release Process - Why It’s Important and How to Be Ready
entry.content:
Release management plays a critical role in ensuring your SAP system is always up to date, stable, and performing optimally. Particularly in the cloud environment, knowing when to expect new innovations and how to adopt them helps businesses prepare for updates, implement new features smoothly, and avoid disruptions in operations. Let's dive deeper into why release management matters and how to do it effectively.

Importance of Release Management for a Cloud Product

In a cloud-based environment, the software is continuously updated. These updates ensure your system is running with the latest features, security patches, and performance improvements. Release management is key because it provides structured and predictable updates that businesses can prepare for.

Unlike on-premise products, where companies control when and how to install updates, cloud-based products operate on automatic update schedules managed by the provider (in this case, SAP). This makes it essential for businesses to stay informed about release cycles to avoid disruptions.

Why Release Management is Critical During Implementation

During implementation, release management becomes even more crucial. Knowing when a new release is scheduled allows project teams to plan around potential downtimes and align project milestones with the release cycle. Ignoring the release schedule could lead to disruptions or delays that can throw off an entire project timeline.

For example, if a significant feature or functionality is released while the team is testing or finalizing system configurations, it could lead to unforeseen issues or require rework. Incorporating release management into your project plan helps avoid these pitfalls by ensuring you're prepared for scheduled changes.

Finding Release Schedules and Enablement Content

Successful Release Management means staying up to date on all the upcoming innovations for the products you are using. The more products you use, the more content you need to keep track of: Release schedules, blog posts, newsletters, videos, podcasts, webcasts, social media channels, webpages, e-learning… there’s a long list of excellent and helpful content out there to inform and enable you but sourcing it for each release can require some time.

For an efficient and convenient way to find all available release information, you can use the release navigators by SAP Enterprise Support.

 

Example release navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

This tool consolidates key resources on feature releases from various SAP teams in one easy-to-use web application per solution area, including:

  • Release dates and schedules
  • Release blogs, webinars, e-learnings, newsletters, videos, podcasts…
  • Resources curated in collaboration with Product Management, Product Engineering, User Assistance, Learning, Enterprise Support, Onboarding…
  • Guided Tours and Navigation Tips to ensure great user experience

Links and content within the release navigators are continuously updated to reflect the latest release.

 

Example release navigator page for SAP Integration Suite within the release navigator for SAP BTP

The release navigator is available to all customers as part of their SAP Enterprise Support offering and to all SAP partners.

The solutions covered include:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • SAP Customer Experience
  • SAP Human Capital Management
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Private Edition coming in HY1 2025)
  • SAP Supply Chain Management
  • Spend Management & Business Network

All release navigators can be accessed via the Release Ready page in the SAP Support Portal.

The Release Ready page within the SAP Support Portal directs users to all available release navigators.

To learn more about the release navigators, we recommend to read the SAP Community blog posts under the tag “Readiness@Scale”.

Release Management Post-Go-Live

After your system goes live, release management doesn’t stop. In fact, it becomes even more important to ensure the long-term stability and performance of your SAP system. Your technical administrator should always be aware of upcoming releases and, if necessary, plan for regression testing before each update.

Equally important is communicating the update schedule to end users so they can prepare for any temporary system unavailability or changes in functionality.

Another important reason for continuous release monitoring is to avoid that users are unaware of a new and improved feature and miss out on significant improvements to their landscape and user experience.

Conclusion

Release management is an integral part of ensuring the smooth functioning of your SAP environment, especially in a cloud setting. By understanding the release process, staying up to date with SAP’s release schedules, and leveraging the tools available like the release navigator, you can effectively plan for updates, mitigate risks, and ensure your system continues to perform at its best.

Keep in mind that effective release management is not a one-time task but an ongoing process—one that helps secure the long-term success of your SAP deployment.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series: Customer Onboarding at SAP.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

Chris is a Release Advisor for SAP BTP at SAP’s Readiness@Scale/Enterprise Support team, specializing in helping customers adopt innovations quickly and efficiently. Chris has been instrumental in the launch and continuous improvement of the Release Navigator for SAP BTP and aligning SAP BTP release processes, e.g. regarding release schedule communication and consistent baseline release information. With a strong background in digital learning for SAP BTP, years of experience in learning and enablement roles as well as an organizational psychology degree, Chris is dedicated to enhancing customer enablement and driving successful outcomes.

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @chriskollhed .

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Fully Autonomous Finance? Is that our Goal? (A Case for Human-in-the-Middle Scenarios)
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving world of finance, AI is transforming how businesses operate. From automating routine tasks like transaction matching to summarizing and analyzing complex data sets including structured and non-structured information, AI offers remarkable efficiencies.

However, the human element remains crucial in the AI process, ensuring that technology serves its intended purpose effectively and ethically, while meeting specific compliance requirements.

Firstly, human oversight is essential for maintaining ethical standards. AI systems can process vast amounts of data, but they lack the ability to understand the nuances of ethical decision-making. Humans provide the moral compass, ensuring that AI applications align with societal values and regulatory requirements. This oversight helps prevent biases and ensures fairness.

SAP’s approach to Business AI addresses the requirements of ethical responsibility in AI – as well as relevance and reliability in AI. SAP has an AI ethics steering committee and an AI ethics advisory panel with industry leaders to ensure the ethical use of AI in the business context. These teams work with our customers for a more ethical use of AI that aligns with our core values.

Secondly, human expertise adds a layer of critical thinking that AI cannot replicate. While AI excels at identifying patterns and trends, it doesn't possess the intuition and judgment that come from years of experience. Financial professionals can interpret AI-generated insights, considering broader economic contexts and existing relationships with business partners and networks. This leads to more informed decisionmaking considering factors that machines alone might miss. It’s then the combination of the human and AI that delivers more value that leads to better decisionmaking.

Example:  Finance experts provide detailed commentary on financial results. AI can generate a summary that combines the commentary with financial analysis, making a well-explained, comprehensive view of business performance available to business managers that may not be financial experts.

In the world of finance, there could be a legal burden of proof when decisions lead to results that destroy shareholder value. For example, in Germany, this applies to both the management boards of publicly traded stock corporations (AG) as well as limited liability companies (GmbH), regardless of their size. In these cases, company officers might be required to show the data they used as a basis for their decisions[1]. This often includes demonstrating how the risks and opportunities were weighed, and that the decisions that were made can be reasonably considered to be “in the best interest of the organization”. If AI is making decisions autonomously, it might be impossible to re-create the data foundation of the decision. This could leave company officers liable to legal consequences.

Moreover, humans play a vital role in balancing risk and rewards, based on professional experience. AI systems, though powerful, are not infallible. They can make errors or be vulnerable to cyber threats. Human involvement ensures continuous monitoring and quick intervention when anomalies occur, safeguarding financial systems from potential disruptions.

AI at SAP: Human-in-the-Middle

At SAP, as AI is embedded into finance business processes, the processes are designed to keep experts involved. In the early days of AI, machine learning provided recommendations for expert intervention based on previous experience – for example suggesting a write-off of a particilar goods-and-invoice-receipt reconciliation issue. The system proposed the “(most-likely) next step” option for moving the process forward. The accountant would then restart the process with a single click.

With the evolution of generative AI, SAP Business AI can propose the mapping of one chart of accounts to a standard chart, suggest configuration settings for confirmation, and even explain the accounting details behind the evolution of asset values over times.

The current focus of many teams currently are AI agents. AI agents act as co-workers for accountants, assisting in real time. The accountant has the experience of having their own dedicated, super-personalized assistant that knows all your data, speeds up tasks, and enhances decision-making.

AI agents handle the most simple processing tasks and summarize decision options for the accountant. In these cases, the accountant is making the key decisions, and the follow-on actions are triggered via workflows that are performed by a combination of real and virtual agents.

In these examples you can see that AI supports and assists the accountant for more effective outcomes. While AI prepares analyses for decisionmaking, it’s the human that makes the decision.

Example: AI can use your company’s specific accounting policy and your sales forecast data to derive accrual postings related to sales commissions. However, rather than post these accruals directly, the system generates a posting proposal which is confirmed by the local accountant and then approved by the accounting manager. The journal posting is triggered only after this principle of dual control is followed. This reduces compliance risk for the CFO who remains personally responsible for the completeness and accuracy of corporate financial reports in many jurisdications. Read this blog for more details.

In conclusion, while AI revolutionizes finance, the human touch remains indispensable. By integrating human insight and control, businesses can harness AI's full potential, ensuring a future where technology and accountants work hand in hand. Accountants are not going to be replaced by AI. Accountants who use AI effectively will replace accountants who don’t.

 

Learn more about SAP Business AI in Finance here: https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/finance.html

 

(And thanks to my colleague Daniel Welzbacher for his input!)

[1] Business Judgement Rule (§93(1)(2) Germany’s Stock Corporation Act (AktG)).

entry.title: Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
This blog shares highlights on Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.

  • Ability To Customize Cancellation Reasons
  • Enhanced Change Capabilities For Mass Update Application For Service Contracts
  • Debit Memo Request For Service Contract Invoices
  • Add Fields to Form Templates for Service Quotation
  • Send Service Quotations for Workflow Approval using OData API

Watch my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

Ability To Customize Cancellation Reasons

One of the standout features in this release is the ability to customize cancellation reasons. In response to numerous customer and partner requests, SAP now enables you to add custom cancellation reasons that reflect more specific insights about why contracts are terminated. This not only aids in understanding customer needs better but also helps in refining service delivery.

You can now choose customized cancellation reasons when you cancel service contracts. This flexibility helps you understand why customers decide to cancel the contracts. Using the cancellation reasons, you can make data-driven improvements.

Key users and configuration experts can now make settings for service contract cancellations with two new configuration activities:

First, you can define the cancelling party. This means you can designate which parties are authorized to cancel a contract.

Second, you can define cancellation reasons. This provides the ability to set and assign specific reasons for contract cancellations to a cancelling party.

Understanding specific cancellation reasons allows businesses to address customer concerns proactively, reducing churn and enhancing service offerings based on informed decision-making. 

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

Demo: Ability to Customize Cancellation Reasons

 

Enhanced Change Capabilities For Mass Update Application For Service Contracts

Building on enhancements introduced in previous releases, 2502 brings robust capabilities for mass updates of service contracts. These include changing partner roles and payment terms/methods en masse, making it easier to adapt to large-scale customer requests or policy changes efficiently.

You can now change billing-relevant data, such as the payment method and terms of payment, at both the header and item levels. In addition, you can change business partners at the header and item levels.

You can change standard partner roles, such as the employee responsible, though this does not include the sold-to party.

You also have the ability to change payment terms and methods efficiently. This update ensures significant time savings and operational efficiency by enabling bulk changes at both the service contract header and item levels. Whether you are adjusting employee responsibilities or modifying payment methods across multiple contracts, these capabilities offer seamless process management.

By automating mass updates, organizations save time and reduce manual errors, increasing operational efficiency and freeing resources for strategic tasks.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

Demo: Enhanced Change Capabilities For Mass Update Application For Service Contracts

 

Debit Memo Request For Service Contract Invoices

The ability to issue debit memo requests for service contract invoices is another feature designed to enhance financial operations. If discrepancies such as unpaid service orders arise or additional services are rendered, you can effortlessly create debit memo requests to notify customers of outstanding payments.

With this feature, you can create debit memo requests that were created with reference to service invoices. A debit memo request is a sales document that can be used to request a debit memo for a customer. Creating debit memo requests makes sense when the price that the customer has paid was too low. This could be due to wrongly calculated prices. After a debit memo request is created, you can find it in the transaction history of the corresponding service transaction. From there, for service contracts only, you can also navigate to relevant apps for more details about the document.

After the debit memo requests are created, they will show up in the Process Flow in the referenced service contracts. 

Figure: Debit Memo Request For Service Contract Invoices


In addition, SAP now allows these debit memo requests to be efficiently uploaded via spreadsheets, catering to businesses dealing with a high volume of transactions. This functionality will streamline operations and ensure timely follow-up on client invoices. Streamlined financial processes improve cash flow management and facilitate prompt customer interactions, enhancing financial transparency and reliability.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

Demo: Debit Memo Request For Service Contract Invoices

 

Add Fields to Form Templates for Service Quotation

The previous standard form template lacks flexibility with the output forms for Service Quotations. The standard template is not designed on S/4HANA Output Management. That makes it difficult to access key information in service quotations. 

Originally, the standard form template uses a non-standard wrapper.  Now it is replaced by a common wrapper created for service objects. The new wrapper can be applied to Service Quotations.

 
With this enhancement, customers are able to add more fields to the form template as output for service quotations. With the new fields in the output form, customers have better clarity in the detailed terms of agreement in the service quotations. This leaves no room for confusion in the complicated quotation processes.

This is an example of the standard output form of a service quotation.

After adding new fields to the output form, the output form looks like the following. 

Send Service Quotations for Workflow Approval using OData API

Before this enhancement, the OData API cannot trigger the approval workflow for service quotations. Customers can only use the button "Send for Approval" in the Manage Service Quotations app to trigger the approval workflow. Lacking the capability in the OData API makes the approval process time consuming.

This was resolved by adding the Action "SubmitForApproval" in the ODATA API for Service Quotation that can be used by external integrations.

The enhancement in the OData API helps customers trigger approval workflow for service quotations when they use integration and custom UI5 Apps. Users do not need to open the Manage Service Quotations app and trigger the workflow manually. It saves you time and effort. 

Here is the documentation for the API: Service Quotation (A2X)

Here is the process flow for the service quotation approval using the OData API.

Figure: Send Quotation for Workflow Approval for Service Quotations using OData API

entry.title: Introduction to SAP S/4HANA Advanced Intercompany Sales
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Advanced Intercompany Sales is a robust solution designed to streamline and optimize the sales processes between affiliated companies within a corporate group. This functionality is particularly useful for organizations where a local sales office sells products to external customers, but the goods are delivered directly from a producing company or a central warehouse. The process ensures seamless integration between sales, procurement, delivery, and billing operations, enabling efficient intercompany transactions.

Business Process Overview

 

The Advanced Intercompany Sales process involves the following key steps:

  1. Sales Order Creation: A standard sales order is created by the selling company for an external customer. The system automatically identifies if the delivering plant belongs to a different company code, making the order relevant for intercompany processing.
  2. Intercompany Purchase Order: Upon saving the sales order, the system generates an intercompany purchase order in the selling company. This purchase order is directed to the delivering company, which is responsible for supplying the goods.
  3. Intercompany Sales Order: The delivering company receives the purchase order and creates an intercompany sales order. This order is used to manage the internal transaction between the delivering company and the selling company.
  4. Delivery and Goods Movement: The delivering company processes the outbound delivery and ships the goods directly to the external customer. The system manages the goods movements, including the transfer of goods into and out of stock in transit, ensuring accurate inventory tracking.
  5. Billing: Two types of invoices are generated:
    • Customer Invoice: The selling company bills the external customer for the goods delivered.
    • Intercompany Customer Invoice: The delivering company bills the selling company for the intercompany transaction.
  6. Invoice Verification: The intercompany supplier invoice is verified against the intercompany customer invoice to ensure accuracy and compliance.

Key Features

  • Real-Time Integration: The process is fully integrated into the SAP S/4HANA value chain monitoring framework, allowing real-time tracking and monitoring of each step.
  • Valuated Stock in Transit: The system uses valuated stock in transit to manage the transfer of goods between affiliated companies, ensuring accurate financial reporting and inventory management.
  • Automated Workflows: Many steps, such as the creation of intercompany purchase orders and sales orders, are automated, reducing manual effort and minimizing errors.

Setup Steps

To enable Advanced Intercompany Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, the following setup steps are required:

  1. Enable the Process: Activate the Advanced Intercompany Sales process (scope item 5D2) through expert configuration. This involves enabling specific pre-delivered configurations.
  2. Assign Purchasing Organization and Group: Assign a purchasing organization and purchasing group to the sales organization of the intercompany sales order.
  3. Configure Pricing: Set up pricing independently for the delivering and selling companies. This includes defining pricing procedures and condition types for internal and external customers.
  4. Define Transfer of Control Dates: Configure the internal and external transfer of control dates to manage the timing of goods movements and revenue recognition.
  5. Monitor Value Chains: Use the Monitor Value Chains app to orchestrate and monitor each step of the intercompany sales process.

Analyzing the Process

To analyze and resolve issues in the Advanced Intercompany Sales process, you can use the following tools:

  • Sales Order Fulfillment - Analyze and Resolve Issues App: This app helps identify and resolve issues that may impede the fulfillment of sales orders. It provides insights into critical stages of the process and facilitates collaboration with relevant contacts.
  • Monitor Value Chains App: This app offers a comprehensive view of the entire intercompany sales process, allowing you to track the status of sales orders, deliveries, and invoices.

Conclusion

SAP S/4HANA Advanced Intercompany Sales provides a comprehensive solution for managing complex intercompany sales processes. By automating key steps and integrating them into a unified framework, it enhances efficiency, accuracy, and visibility across the supply chain. Proper setup and configuration, along with the use of analytical tools, ensure smooth operation and timely resolution of any issues that may arise.

entry.title: Staffing Facet in Project Control - Professional Services Projects App (F6869)
entry.content:
Great news! You no longer need to open another app to view the staffing details for your projects. Until now you had to use the Staffing Analysis app for these details. All this information is now available within the Project Control - Professional Services Projects app.

We've introduced information about project staffing within the app Project Control - Professional Services Projects. The Staffing facet provides comprehensive information related to resource requests. This includes work package details, work items, roles, requested and staffed efforts, worker and staffing details, delivery organization, billing control category, and work package type. Additional details such as costing data and billing element details are also available.

For example, you can highlight if staffing has been cost effective by comparing the costs that you had planned and the staffing cost of the resource.

Another new feature is that you can group the staffing for different billing elements which allows you to see who is working on which contract items. (for example, fixed price and T&M)

 

This facet also supports staffing done within external staffing solutions like SAP Project and Resource Management (6JO). If there are multiple workers staffed to a resource request, you can easily view the breakdown of the staffed efforts and the staffed resource organizations.

If you have not been using the app Project Control – Professional Services Projects, please take a look at it on your home page, on the Fiori launchpad.

Here are few additional details:

 Fiori App Ref library: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6869')/S34

App Help :   https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/db25b660143d4ffbad8e7f4825b9d83e.html?locale=en-US

 If you have additional feedback on the app, please submit them here. https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/930

entry.title: Education & Research in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
New Year, New Innovations!

Driving Innovation in Education & Research with the new Release 2502 of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

Dear SAP Education & Research Cloud ERP enthusiasts, 

As we stepped into 2025, the world of technology, and specifically AI, continues to evolve at an amazing pace, ushering in new opportunities and challenges for industries across the board. For Education & Research institutions, staying ahead means embracing cutting-edge innovations that drive operational efficiency, get the most out of research funds, and enhance the experience of academic and research staff, and finance departments. The 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, is designed to do just that.

This release represents a significant milestone in the journey toward an even more intelligent, cloud-based ERP tailored to the unique needs of Education & Research. With an expanded focus on AI capabilities, including the enhanced functionality of SAP Joule, our AI-powered co-pilot, institutions can leverage smarter, more intuitive tools to navigate a dynamic landscape with agility and confidence. SAP Joule now brings even more powerful insights, and delivers personalized, data-driven recommendations to staff working on research projects, grants, budgets, and finance, enabling them to focus on strategic priorities rather than operational complexities.

In this blog, we’ll explore the key highlights of the 2502 release, focusing on how these advancements empower Education & Research organizations to meet their strategic goals. Let’s dive into the details and discover how SAP continues to shape the future of public cloud ERP for this fantastic sector!

Cloud ERP core enhancements

Before we dive into the innovations within Grants & Budget Management please find below a range of new core Cloud ERP feature-based innovations for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the 2502 release.

Out of a wide availability of enhancements, I have selected three topics that I believe are very relevant for institutions of Education and Research: AI/UX, Sustainability, and Procurement. If you are interested in other areas of innovations for SAP’s Cloud ERP, please check out the Release Video from Bert Schulze:


Screenshot + URL: Get the top highlights of the 2502 release in this video by Bert Schulze

AI-Powered UX with SAP Joule

Joule, our Gen AI co-pilot, transforms user experience by simplifying tasks with instant insights and seamless navigation using natural language input.
A standout feature is its personalized card creation, allowing users to quickly access critical ERP data like purchase orders and delivery updates, empowering more informed decision-making, and insights in for example the essential goods required for running a smooth research project.


Screenshot/Video URL: Get instant insights based on natural language interaction with Joule.

 
Screenshot/Video URL: AI-assisted smart summarization


Screenshot/Video URL: AI-assisted easy filtering 

As we advance in AI-assisted UX capabilities, the upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release brings exciting innovations in BETA testing, including:

  • AI-assisted easy enterprise search
  • AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home
  • and AI-assisted error explanation

You can sign up through this link to start your BETA testing!

Enhancing Carbon Accountability with SAP’s Green Ledger

SAP Green Ledger is a groundbreaking financial solution central to carbon accounting. It seamlessly integrates with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability platforms, extending traditional financial accounting to include carbon measurements. By applying double-entry accounting principles to carbon quantities, it enables businesses to track these on dimensions relevant to operational management, such as profit centers and segments. This approach provides a clear view of carbon impact from provisioning to delivery.

The core value of SAP Green Ledger lies in its ability to monitor, quantify, and report the carbon footprint across end-to-end operations, delivering auditable and reliable reports. By combining carbon data with financial metrics, it provides actionable insights essential for informed and sustainable decision-making.


Screenshot: SAP Green Ledger integrates seamlessly with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability.

Expanding the “Manage Certificate Receipts” App with Create Mode

The S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release enhances the "Manage Certificate Receipts" app with a new Create Mode. Previously, the app allowed users to manage and link quality certificates to purchasing documents and goods receipts, improving compliance and traceability. The new Create Mode adds flexibility by enabling users to record certificates received before goods arrive, such as those received via email, and link them to material records upon arrival.

 
Screenshot: Record certificate receipts before goods arrival, ensuring smoother inventory processes.

Grants, Budget, and Research Project Management

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2502 release delivers a robust suite of Grants and Budget Management features tailored for public sector entities, including state and local governments, cities, K-12 schools, and Education & Research institutions.

Building upon previous releases, the 2502 update introduces a range of new capabilities designed to support Education & Research customers. Below is an overview of the latest advancements, including related innovations for Enterprise Project & Portfolio Management.

For more details on this new global PSM scope bundle, visit the SAP Process Navigator to explore the complete list of available scope items. Direct link to the Roadmap Explorer: URL


Screenshot: Process Navigator focused on S/4HANA Public Cloud, and the PSM industry capabilities.

Additional countries 

With the 2502 release, the Public Sector Management (PSM) scope bundle is now available for US (GASB), Austria, Japan, India, Singapore, New Zealand, Belgium, and Switzerland, alongside the existing countries: the US, Germany, Canada, Australia, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

This industry-specific scope bundle, and functionalities, developed in collaboration with Education & Research institutions, offer tailored processes required for the financial management of sponsored research, grants, and funds/budgets. It also includes core functionalities for finance, procurement, logistics, asset accounting, treasury, leasing, sales, plant maintenance, contract accounts, and (grant-integrated) project management.

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: Roadmap Release overview

New employee financing using funds commitments

Overview

  • Leverage the personnel encumbrances in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud through the creation of earmarked documents
  • Enable financing of employees with their corresponding funding sources (funds, grants, or functional areas) which will allow the monitoring and control of budget consumption.
  • Budget availability checks when financing a new employee based on employee theoretical costs.

Benefits

  • Enable accurate budget monitoring and control of personnel expenses by allowing the financing of employees in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central.
  • Help ensure budget availability for payroll expenses throughout the financial year, including refinancing of employees when salary changes

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: Position budgeting Control in SuccessFactors with cost objects from SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition

Additional flexibility in maintaining project milestones

 Overview:

  • Enables direct maintenance of project milestones within the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS).
  • Allows users to create milestones via the user interface or API for seamless integration.
  • Supports detailed milestone definitions for improved planning and execution.

Benefits:

  • Provides additional granularity in defining and monitoring project progress.
  • Facilitates milestone creation through APIs, enhancing system interoperability.
  • Ensures clear, well-documented milestones for audits and reporting.

 Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: maintaining milestones for research projects.

Additional items in the project cost report

Overview:

  • Displays commitments for purchase requisitions and inventory costs directly in the project cost report.
  • Provides both aggregated and non-aggregated cost views for better financial analysis.
  • Links procurement and inventory costs to project financials for a comprehensive overview.

Benefits:

  • Enhances visibility into project-related expenditures across procurement and inventory.
  • Captures costs at various logistic execution stages, ensuring precise project budgeting.
  • Provides clear documentation of project costs for audits and financial oversight.

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: research project cost report example

Residual-budget processing for Grants

Overview:

  • Enables either lapse or carry forward of unspent grant funds to the next fiscal year.
  • Ensures consistency by extending residual budget processing to grants, not just general budgets.
  • Maintains clear records of carried-forward amounts for transparency and reporting.

Benefits:

  • Prevents loss of unspent funds, optimizing resource allocation.
  • Provides better forecasting and control over multi-year grant spending.
  • Enable users to restrict future reporting of grant budgets on the current fiscal year vs reporting back into past fiscal years.

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: set up a residual budget transfer for Grants.

 
Screenshot: successful run of residual budget transfer process

Output services for Earmarked Funds

Overview:

  • Easily create and export earmarked fund documents in a standardized PDF format.
  • Ensures earmarked fund records are stored electronically for easy retrieval and audit.
  • Generates legally compliant documents with necessary approvals and timestamps.

Benefits:

  • Ensures earmarked fund documents meet regulatory and audit requirements.
  • Enables quick access, retrieval, and long-term digital archiving of financial records.
  • Prevents document tampering and ensures authenticity with standardized PDF outputs.

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: creation of the Earmarked Fund and the PDF output document.


Screenshot: PDF output from the Earmarked Fund Document

Staffing Resources in Projects Using Project and Resource Management  

Overview:

  • Enables project managers to create detailed staffing requests with skill, availability, and timeline specifications.
  • Uses intelligent algorithms to match employees to projects based on skill fit and availability.
  • Connects SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Project and Resource Management for streamlined staffing operations.

Benefits:

  • Ensures the right resources are allocated to projects efficiently through AI-assisted matching.
  • Provides clear documentation of resource assignments for compliance and audit purposes.
  • Reduces manual effort with seamless integration, visualization, and filtering for resource allocation.


Screenshot: matching resources to staff a research project.

Key Summary

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition latest 2502 release brings key enhancements, including AI-driven user experiences, embedded AI features, improved Grants and Budget Management capabilities, and the innovative SAP Green Ledger for integrated carbon accounting. And last but not least the ABAP Cloud Add-Ons also enables partners to extend and differentiate solutions effectively.

This release continues to drive efficiency, scalability, and sustainability in ERP operations for institutions of Education & Research. Please join us in the Cloud ERP revolution and explore how these innovations can unlock your institution's full potential. Stay tuned for deeper insights in upcoming blog posts, and engage with experts on our SAP Cloud ERP community page.

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21st and January 24th, we conducted a series of engaging live sessions to showcase the exciting innovations in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

 Find All of Our Assets   

The 2502 release showcases 185 innovation highlights across the portfolio. 
Check out our release document for all the details!


Screenshot: Key Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

Thank you!

 Rob Jonkers - SAP Education & Research

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley highlighted how AI makes app development easier
entry.content:
Thanks to all the local customers who joined us for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley on February 5, 2025. By the end of the day, everyone saw the latest and greatest AI innovations that guide business users through their SAP tasks more quickly and make developers more productive by automating and accelerating key parts of their work.

For many, the highlight was the hands-on session where they had the opportunity to build an SAP Fiori app, starting with a sketch of what they want the app to look like. They learned about generative AI, the Flexible Programming Model in SAP Fiori elements, and how to fine-tune apps using SAP Fiori tools.

My favorite part of the event were the conversations with customers during the breaks where I had a chance to learn more about their specific industries and the unique challenges they face. This reinforces the need for some level of customization in the starting page, induvial apps, and business processes.

Schedule for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley

The day started with an overview of SAP Fiori and what this looks like in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Similar to how you would begin your day with SAP, we started with My Home and all the different ways you have to launch your SAP Fiori apps and make the page your own.

Graphic showing personalization options in My Home by creating the cards you want to see

We then showed examples of the SAP Fiori user experience across SAP S/4HANA, including many of the innovations that we have recently added to our public cloud version. We also presented several ways on how to create cards to show key information in My Home.

SAP Fiori includes more AI capabilities for business users

In the morning, we showed all the released AI functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, much of which we introduced in 2502.

  • Easy filter – natural language searching in SAP Fiori elements list reports
  • Smart summarization – AI-created summary of SAP Fiori elements object pages
  • Card creation in My Home– several ways to create a card that you can add to My Home
  • Joule-assisted insight card creation in My Home– ask Joule for the information you need and create a card that you can add to My Home

We ended the day with some previews of planned AI functionality that will first be released into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

  • Recap – a summary of what you missed when you were away from your SAP system
  • Assisted error resolution – providing more context on system errors with recommendations to resolve them
  • Microsoft CoPilot integration

Some of these are currently in beta testing with selected public cloud customers.

Clean core is how you can extend your SAP S/4HANA system.

One important area that many customers are interested in hearing about is how to extend their SAP S/4HANA systems. This could be creating new apps or moving existing customizations from their on-prem environments into their private cloud implementation. There are many options, some on-stack in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and some on the side in BTP.

Graphic of the options to extend your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system in a clean core way

RISE extensibility guide

GROW extensibility guide

The partners shared their vison for the future and how to get there

Event gold sponsors cbs, Lemongrass, and Qubittron presented about various use cases for AI and how they have helped customers overcome initial hesitation. They also shared their wisdom in a panel discussion that covered different dimensions of UX transformation along with SAP Fiori adoption, change management, and how to gain alignment among different stakeholders in an organization.

Thanks to everyone who made SAP Fiori Innovation Day a success

SAP Presenters (in order of appearance): Sebastian Steinhauer, Katja Zoch, Chad Leonard, Vikas Lodha, Dan Watters, Carolina Robertson, Sushant Priyadarshi, Conrad Bernal, Ashley Tung, Marcel Kassner

Gold partner sponsors: Qubittron, cbs Corporate Business Solutions, lemongrass

Silver partner sponsor: qBotica

Behind the scenes team: all the developers, architects, product managers and other colleagues who built the functionality and set up the demos and hands-on systems.

Without all the great customer interaction, questions, and conversations during meals and breaks, we would not have learned what’s most important to you as you move forward in each of your respective organizations in your UX transformation projects.

 

Next steps

  • Register for the monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable call to meet other developers and architects who build SAP Fiori apps.
  • Subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter, in which we share updates on SAP Fiori elements, SAP Fiori tools, SAPUI5, and SAP Build Code every other month.  
  • Read about last year’s SAP Fiori Innovation Days events in Heidelberg, Chicago, and Dublin.
  • Join us at an SAP Fiori Development Innovation Day in 2025 – we are finalizing the locations and dates.
